description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by three wide, weathered metal hoops. The wood displays a natural grain pattern, with some staves showing subtle variations in color and texture, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top and bottom are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top surface flush and unadorned. The metal hoops are slightly rusted, adding to the rustic, utilitarian appearance. One side of the barrel features a dark stain, possibly from liquid spillage, indicating prior use. There are no visible handles, spigots, or markings, and the overall look is robust and functional, suitable for storage or transport in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with three metal hoops, natural wood grain, and a dark stain on one side, exuding a rustic, utilitarian feel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wood grain, three metal hoops, and a clean, unstained surface.
description_view_1: Side view with visible wood texture and metal hoops, no significant stains or markings.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 1, with consistent wood and metal features.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring a prominent dark stain on the lower wood staves, with all three metal hoops visible.
